behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between manifest parser, transitive dependency resolver, and vulnerability-check input assembly. Required setup: wire real parser+resolver modules together with fixture manifests/lockfiles and assert the assembled check set contains both direct and transitive deps. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because this behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; in-process module wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency collection against fixtures containing direct and nested transitive deps (e.g., npm/yarn lock or pip tree fixture), then assert the vulnerability-check request payload includes every expected package@version from both levels with no omissions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error at the real vulnerability API contract boundary (query encoding/interpretation of version ranges). Required setup: call the real vulnerability API over HTTP with controlled package/version-range cases and verify returned CVEs correspond to the exact manifest ranges. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because a stubbed API can mask real provider-side range parsing/contract mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a test manifest with known package names across multiple version ranges, allow it to hit the real vulnerability database endpoint, and assert the returned CVE set differs correctly by range (same package name, different vulnerable windows).

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in deterministic comparator/sort function. Required setup: call sort logic with synthetic CVE inputs covering all severities and tie-break CVSS values. Rejection gate: Integration/System are disqualified because behavior text is pure ordering logic with no external boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted CVE list into the sort function and assert final order is severity rank (Critical>High>Medium>Low), then descending CVSS within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between vulnerability fix selector and manifest read/modify/write module (unintended edits to unrelated entries). Required setup: run patch-generation path on realistic manifest fixtures and compare before/after structure/content for non-target keys. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because real OS/network behavior is not required; local in-process file/content handling catches the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Apply remediation for a single vulnerable dependency on a fixture manifest, then assert only that dependency version changed and every other dependency entry/value is byte- or AST-equivalent.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error at Git hosting API boundary (auth, branch/push/PR creation, rendered diff correctness). Required setup: exercise real repository remote/API and verify resulting PR and diff in the hosting platform. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by explicit behavior text: "created on the real repository."
behaviors.test_description: Against a disposable real repo, run scanner remediation for one vulnerable dependency and assert a PR is opened remotely, includes exactly one dependency version change, and no unrelated file changes.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in semver comparison/classification. Required setup: direct function tests over representative version pairs (including pre-release/build metadata cases as needed). Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is a deterministic semver rule, not an external interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver classifier with version transitions (e.g., 1.9.4->2.0.0, 0.9.0->1.0.0 per chosen policy) and assert breaking-change flag is true only for major bumps under the project’s semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module orchestration/wiring error between semver decision logic, PR creation service, and report/output layer. Required setup: wire these modules with a fake PR gateway and verify both side effects (no PR call + review flag present). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI output dependency; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner flow on a fixture that triggers a major bump, assert PR client is not invoked, and assert scanner output/report contains the expected review-required flag/reason.
